content.problemStatement:
The zombie apocalypse is here and Chef is stuck at an old construction site. The site is surrounded by walls on three sides. On the fourth side is a fence where NNN zombies are standing in a row. The zombies are numbered from 111 to NNN from left to right. Zombie iii has ZiZ_iZi‚Äã health points initially.

Luckily there are MMM cranes Chef has access to. The ithi^{th}ith crane carries KiK_iKi‚Äã construction beams. Chef can drop any number ‚â•0\ge 0‚â•0 and ‚â§Ki\le K_i‚â§Ki‚Äã of these beams from crane iii and they will hit all zombies whose numbers lie between LiL_iLi‚Äã to RiR_iRi‚Äã both inclusive. A single beam decreases the health of all zombies it hits by 111 point. If a zombie's health equals 000 after being hit it is considered dead.

Can Chef kill all the zombies using the beams? If yes, what is the minimum number of beams that Chef has to drop to achieve this?

**Warning: Large I/O, it is advisable to use fast I/O methods.**

Input:
The first line contains TTT, number of testcases.
The first line of each test case contains two integers NNN and MMM.
The second line of each test case contains NNN integers Z1,Z2,...,ZNZ_1, Z_2, ..., Z_NZ1‚Äã,Z2‚Äã,...,ZN‚Äã.
MMM lines follow. The ithi^{th}ith line contains 333 integers LiL_iLi‚Äã, RiR_iRi‚Äã and KiK_iKi‚Äã.
Output:
For each test case, if it is possible for Chef to kill all the zombies print a single line containing "**YES**" followed by the minimum number of beams he needs to drop. If it is impossible, print "**NO**" instead. (Note that the judge is case-sensitive)
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases ‚â§3‚ãÖ105\leq 3 \cdot 10^5‚â§3‚ãÖ105
Sum of MMM over all test cases ‚â§3‚ãÖ105\leq 3 \cdot 10^5‚â§3‚ãÖ105
**Case 1**: One possible way is for Chef to drop the only beam from crane 2, 1 beam from crane 3, 1 beam from crane 4 and all 3 beams from crane 5.
**Case 2**: Zombie 1 and 3 can be killed but zombie 2 remains at health 1 even if all the beams are dropped.
